name: Semantic Router (LLM Input Classification)

auth.notes: Encoder API keys (OpenAI, Cohere, HuggingFace) for embeddings

performance.notes: Embedding-based routing; much faster than LLM-based routing

agent_readiness.notes: Route LLM inputs to handlers based on semantic similarity. Route objects with utterance examples. RouteLayer(encoder, routes). router('user query') returns matched Route. Encoders: OpenAI, Cohere, FastEmbed (local), HuggingFace. Dynamic routes (call LLM for dynamic responses). Static routes (deterministic returns). Async support. Use cases: topic detection, intent classification, guardrails, chatbot routing, tool selection. Much faster than LLM classification for high-traffic routing. Hybrid semantic + keyword routing.

gotchas: Requires embedding API or local model for encoder
Route accuracy depends on quality of utterance examples
Not a replacement for complex intent classification (LLM better for nuanced cases)
best_when: High-traffic chatbot routing needing sub-100ms classification
LLM guardrails for topic filtering and topic detection
Agent tool selection by semantic similarity
Replacing slow LLM-based routing with embedding-based routing
avoid_when: Complex multi-label intent classification (LLM more accurate)
Environments with no embedding API access
summary: Semantic Router classifies LLM inputs by semantic similarity to route utterances — much faster than LLM-based routing at 10-100ms. Define Routes with examples, RouteLayer routes queries to matching handlers. Encoders: OpenAI, Cohere, FastEmbed (local). Use cases: chatbot routing, guardrails, agent tool selection, topic filtering. Best for high-traffic scenarios where LLM classification latency is unacceptable.
